name: K10 - San Diego
author: k10-san-diego
_about: A single drivable LOOP around Lake Murray (Kevin's childhood neighborhood). This repo is the standalone, Blender-first home for the track: the loop is mapped from the REAL roads (OpenStreetMap) at REAL elevation (USGS 3DEP), imported into Blender, and shaped there interactively (Kevin designs, Claude operates) BEFORE any kn5 export. Start point = just the 5-road main loop; everything else is added deliberately, in Blender.

source._doc: The loop is stitched from the named roads below along the real OSM drivable network (route.stitch='network'), then sampled for real elevation. data/network.cache.json is a cached OSM fetch so re-derivation never depends on a live Overpass call.

layouts._doc: K10 - San Diego: the Lake Murray loop (Del Cerro sub-loop + per-direction carriageways). Spawns on the loop.
The San Diego freeway network (I-5/CA-52/CA-163/I-8, every ramp). SAME kn5 as 'full' — select this to spawn on the freeway (~12 km west). A road connection between the two comes later.

terrain._doc: Lessons carried over from the prior build. Roads and kerbs stay TIGHT to their real sampled elevation. The grass terrain is clamped so the raw DEM's Cowles-area peaks can't tower over a neighborhood loop where there are none.

scenery._doc: palm_roads: names (matching route.roads / OSM) whose verges get tall Mexican fan palms, dropped every palm_spacing_m on both sides. Now the whole loop (all 5 roads) after the Lake Murray Blvd test strip was approved.
scenery._scenery_doc: Off-road only: shade trees populate the near ring past the road edge (the yards), scrub the open ground further out. Placement is gated on distance-to-nearest-road so nothing lands on the drivable surface.

ui_description: K10 - San Diego: a real-world Assetto Corsa track built from OpenStreetMap + USGS 3DEP. Drive the 13.5 km Lake Murray loop around the reservoir (Kevin's childhood neighborhood) — now with the Del Cerro sub-loop and true per-direction elevations on Navajo & College — plus the full San Diego central freeway network (I-5 / CA-52 / CA-163 / I-8, every ramp, on real elevated structure) in one map.
